# CodeAnt AI: YC Rejection-to-Acceptance Story

- Type: LinkedIn founder anecdote
- Status: External unverified story; company YC status verified via official YC directory
- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/amartya-jha_story-how-we-turned-yc-rejection-into-acceptance-share-7168928420494499841-RQpS/
- Verification URL: https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/codeant-ai
- Author: Amartya Jha, as shown in pasted text
- Published: Unknown; story dates are 2023-10-23 through 2023-11-02
- Captured: 2026-06-16
- Method: pasted public LinkedIn text; official YC directory page checked on 2026-06-16
- Caveat: references/caveats/external-unverified-story.md

## Summary

- CodeAnt AI is listed in YC's official company directory as a Winter 2024 company. The page lists CodeAnt AI as active and lists Amartya Jha and Chinmay Bharti as active founders.
- Use this as an external anecdote for interview clarity, post-rejection founder morale, and the importance of explaining product, metrics, growth, and differentiation clearly.
- Do not frame this as YC policy that replying to a rejection email will reopen a decision. The useful pattern is substantive clarification, not persistence for its own sake.

## Content

### LinkedIn Post Text

Story - How we turned YC rejection into acceptance in just one week!

On October 23, 2023, we had our Y Combinator interview scheduled. This was my fourth application to YC, having been rejected three times before with different ideas and co-founders. For the first time, I received a call for an interview.

Chinmay and I reached out to every YC founder we knew, getting advice on what to say, how to say it, and what to avoid. - You should not do it, be real.

Tom and Tyler took our interview, and it didn't go well. I still can't remember those 10 minutes; simply put, we didn't explain our product well.

October 25 at 12:30 AM - We received an email from Tom stating that we were not selected. It was really heartbreaking, but the best part about YC is that they provide reasons for rejection.

After reading the email, it was clear - our YC partners didn't grasp what we were building because we didn't explain our product and metrics clearly.

I told Chinmay, "Let's email them back." Chinmay warned about getting blacklisted, so we quickly called a few YC alumni; all of them said it won't matter, YC partners won't reply after rejection, but YC was our dream, and we weren't giving up even after rejection.

In the next 40 minutes, we made a video of our entire product, added data points about current usage, metrics, and growth. We explained how we stood out from the competition and replied to that rejection email, and slept.

October 26 at 11 PM, got an email from Tom, "Thanks for the reply, guys. I'm going to arrange a second interview with one of my colleagues next week."

We couldn't believe it; no one around us believed it. YC alumni said they had never seen something like this before.

We made sure that in this interview, we focused solely on discussing the product and metrics; we were prepared.

November 1, 2023 - The interview day. I entered the Zoom call and saw Michael Seibel. For 30 seconds, I was starstruck. This was the guy I always wanted to see, and there he was, right in front of me. The interview started, and for the next 8 minutes and 30 seconds, we just talked about the product.

November 2nd, 2023, at 5 AM - We got an email from Tom - "Can you give me a call when you're awake?" Chinmay saw this email first; he called me, and I called Tom. He said, "CodeAnt AI (YC W24) is accepted in YC." - I can't explain it, this was the best day.

We learned one thing, no matter how low the chance of success is, never lose hope and give your best; great things will happen.
